summary: Legacy static Workbench assets have been deleted from the current runtime tree after the mounted Next.js App Router served-route, frontend-acceptance, browser-events, scheduler writeback, and shell gates replaced the old desktop.html/mobile.html/workbench.js/styles.css surface. The API service rejects legacy static opt-in inputs and the old frontend-acceptance CLI is fail-closed; helper exports remain only so current tests can build validation artifacts.

evidence: test/workbench-server.test.js: workbench server is API-only for page routes and rejects retired legacy static opt-in

tools/workbench-static-routes.mjs.project_mount_root: 404 JSON: workbench pages are served by Next.js

acceptance_gate_dependencies.legacy_static_requirement: none; full browser-events closeout replays the success/failure/provider/scheduler/lifecycle/projected-loop/mobile scenarios through the mounted Next.js App Router runtime and mounted API rewrite
none; primary frontend-acceptance runs against the mounted Next.js App Router runtime and reuses the existing artifact validator
none; direct CLI execution exits non-zero with retired status, while buildArtifact and parseAcceptanceOptions remain exported for current validator tests
none; fixture-level artifact tests use mounted Next route paths and durable release evidence
none; resource-readiness artifact tests use mounted Next route paths and mounted favicon evidence
none; verifies scheduler dispatch writeback through mounted Next.js App Router desktop and mobile viewports while preserving strict raw projection pass/3-step assertions

none; asserts Next.js/Ant Design shell source contract, mounted runtime replacement gates, operation readouts, projection API/hook contracts, and responsive overflow checks without reading retired static assets

next_served_route_replacement_gates.replaces_requirement: Next.js Workbench served route verified at /projects/ai-control-platform/ for desktop and mobile viewports
Next browser-events mounted runtime and API writeback probe verified

Full browser-events closeout replay migrated from legacy desktop.html/mobile.html to the mounted Next.js App Router runtime while preserving lifecycle cleanup, projected loop, partial reviewer shard, terminal next-action, resume, mobile, and failure-writeback scenarios.

Primary frontend-acceptance closeout gate migrated from legacy desktop.html/mobile.html to the mounted Next.js App Router runtime with the existing frontend-acceptance validator and durable release evidence.
Scheduler dispatch writeback browser verification migrated from legacy desktop.html/mobile.html to mounted Next.js App Router desktop and mobile viewports.

Current shell gate migrated from legacy desktop.html/mobile.html/workbench.js/styles.css DOM/CSS invariants to Next.js App Router shell source contracts plus mounted runtime replacement-gate assertions.

retirement.reason: P6.1-P6.3 replacement gates passed before deletion. P6.4 removed legacy static files from the runtime tree, removed the legacy npm acceptance script, rejected service-level legacy static opt-in, and removed the deleted large files from .largefile-manifest.json.
completed_evidence: FRONTEND_REFACTOR_CONSTRAINTS.md and FRONTEND_MIGRATION_INVENTORY.md updated to remove fallback-retention requirements
tools/workbench-server.mjs and tools/workbench-static-routes.mjs reject or omit legacy static serving
legacy frontend-acceptance CLI retired and exits non-zero
legacy static serving rejected by test/workbench-server.test.js
apps/workbench/workbench.js and apps/workbench/styles.css removed from .largefile-manifest.json after deletion
